title: GitHub Review by greptile-apps[bot]
summary: The reviewer identifies two main issues in the CI workflow: (1) the smoke-test steps (both inline and in the separate job) hardcode `keystone-manage` instead of deriving the command from `${{ matrix.service }}`, which will break when the matrix expands beyond keystone; and (2) the matrix output silently captures only the last service's image ref, so the separate `smoke-test` job will only validate one image when multiple services are built. A minor robustness fix is also suggested: pipe `wc -l` through `xargs` to trim potential leading whitespace that could produce invalid Docker tags on non-Linux platforms.

code_comments.body:
**Hardcoded `keystone-manage` breaks matrix expansion**

Both this inline PR smoke-test step and the separate `smoke-test` job (line 193) hardcode `keystone-manage --version`. When the `service` matrix is extended beyond `[keystone]`, every run will attempt `keystone-manage --version` regardless of which service is actually being tested â€” causing failures for any non-keystone service. The documentation (`docs/reference/build-images-workflow.md`) already calls this out explicitly, but neither step uses `${{ matrix.service }}` to derive the correct management command.

A robust approach would be to map each service to its management command, or at minimum document it as a required manual step before matrix expansion:

```suggestion
      - name: Smoke test (PR)
        if: github.event_name == 'pull_request'
        env:
          IMAGE_REF: ${{ steps.tags.outputs.composite }}
          MATRIX_SERVICE: ${{ matrix.service }}
        run: docker run --rm "$IMAGE_REF" "${MATRIX_SERVICE}-manage" --version
```

The documentation (`docs/reference/build-images-workflow.md`) already calls this out explicitly, but neither step uses `${{ matrix.service }}` to derive the correct management command.

A robust approach would be to map each service to its management command, or at minimum document it as a required manual step before matrix expansion:

```suggestion

        if: github.event_name == 'pull_request'
        env:

```

code_comments.author: greptile-apps[bot]
code_comments.resolution: [0] Fixed: Added `MATRIX_SERVICE: ${{ matrix.service }}` to the PR smoke test env block and changed the run command from hardcoded `keystone-manage --version` to `"${MATRIX_SERVICE}-manage" --version`, enabling dynamic dispatch per matrix service.

code_comments.body:
**Matrix output captures only the last service â€” `smoke-test` validates only one image**

The comment already acknowledges the limitation, but it's worth flagging the downstream impact clearly: the separate `smoke-test` job (lines 188â€“193) pulls `needs.build-service-images.outputs.image-ref`, which will silently be whichever matrix entry ran last. When the matrix grows to e.g. `service: [keystone, nova]`, both services are built but only one is smoke-tested on push events.

The inline PR smoke test (step at line 168) is unaffected â€” it runs once per matrix cell because it's inside the matrix job itself.

For the separate `smoke-test` job to cover all services, a `fromJSON` output aggregation step is needed before the `outputs:` block, e.g.:

```yaml
# After all matrix steps, collect all image refs into a JSON array:
- name: Collect image ref
  id: collect
  run: echo "image-refs=$(echo "${{ steps.tags.outputs.composite }}")" >> "$GITHUB_OUTPUT"
```

And the `smoke-test` job would iterate over the JSON array. Until then, expanding the matrix will leave most built images untested on push.

When the matrix grows to e.g. `service: [keystone, nova]`, both services are built but only one is smoke-tested on push events.

For the separate `smoke-test` job to cover all services, a `fromJSON` output aggregation step is needed before the `outputs:` block, e.g.:

```yaml
# After all matrix steps, collect all image refs into a JSON array:
- name: Collect image ref
  id: collect
  run: echo "image-refs=$(echo "${{ steps.tags.outputs.composite }}")" >> "$GITHUB_OUTPUT"
```

And the `smoke-test` job would iterate over the JSON array. Until then, expanding the matrix will leave most built images untested on push.

How can I resolve this? If you propose a fix, please make it concise.
`````
</details>
code_comments.author: greptile-apps[bot]
code_comments.resolution: [1] Fixed: Restructured the `smoke-test` job to use its own matrix strategy (`service: [keystone]`, `release: ["2025.2"]`) mirroring `build-service-images`. Added a checkout step and a 'Derive image ref' step that independently reconstructs the composite tag from `source-refs.yaml`, patch count, branch, and SHA â€” eliminating dependency on the single-valued `image-ref` output. Updated the `outputs` comment on `build-service-images` to note the smoke-test job derives its own refs and does not consume this output.

code_comments.body:
**`wc -l` can produce leading whitespace, invalidating Docker tags**

`wc -l` output format differs across platforms: on macOS/BSD it pads the count with leading spaces (e.g., `       3`), while GNU coreutils on Ubuntu omits the padding when reading from stdin. The GHA runner uses Ubuntu so this is not a current runtime issue, but if the script is ever run locally on macOS or tested in a different environment the resulting `PATCH_COUNT` would contain spaces, producing an invalid Docker tag like `keystone:28.0.0-p       3-main-abc1234`.

Trim the output for robustness:

```suggestion
          if [ -d "$PATCH_DIR" ]; then
            PATCH_COUNT=$(find "$PATCH_DIR" -name '*.patch' -type f | wc -l | xargs)
```

<details><summary>Prompt To Fix With AI</summary>

The GHA runner uses Ubuntu so this is not a current runtime issue, but if the script is ever run locally on macOS or tested in a different environment the resulting `PATCH_COUNT` would contain spaces, producing an invalid Docker tag like `keystone:28.0.0-p       3-main-abc1234`.

Trim the output for robustness:

```

How can I resolve this? If you propose a fix, please make it concise.
`````

code_comments.author: greptile-apps[bot]
code_comments.resolution: [2] Fixed: Added `| xargs` to trim `wc -l` output in the `build-service-images` 'Derive tags' step (`PATCH_COUNT=$(find ... | wc -l | xargs)`). The same `| xargs` trimming was also applied in the new `smoke-test` 'Derive image ref' step, which contains an identical patch-counting pattern.

code_comments.body:
**Smoke-test job also hardcodes `keystone-manage`**

Mirroring the issue at line 172, this job-level smoke test also hardcodes `keystone-manage --version`. Since `IMAGE_REF` is already resolved from the matrix output, the service name is embedded in it, but there's no dynamic dispatch to the correct management command.

```suggestion
      - name: Pull and test image
        env:
          IMAGE_REF: ${{ needs.build-service-images.outputs.image-ref }}
        run: |
          docker pull "$IMAGE_REF"
          SERVICE=$(echo "$IMAGE_REF" | sed 's|.*/\([^:]*\):.*|\1|')
          docker run --rm "$IMAGE_REF" "${SERVICE}-manage" --version
```

<details><summary>Prompt To Fix With AI</summary>

`````markdown

```suggestion

        env:
          IMAGE_REF: ${{ needs.build-service-images.outputs.image-ref }}
        run: |
          docker pull "$IMAGE_REF"
          SERVICE=$(echo "$IMAGE_REF" | sed 's|.*/\([^:]*\):.*|\1|')
          docker run --rm "$IMAGE_REF" "${SERVICE}-manage" --version
```

How can I resolve this? If you propose a fix, please make it concise.
`````
</details>
code_comments.author: greptile-apps[bot]
code_comments.resolution: [3] Fixed: The `smoke-test` job's 'Pull and test image' step now uses `MATRIX_SERVICE: ${{ matrix.service }}` env var and runs `"${MATRIX_SERVICE}-manage" --version` instead of hardcoded `keystone-manage --version`. Additionally, `IMAGE_REF` now comes from `${{ steps.tags.outputs.image-ref }}` (the job's own derivation step) instead of `${{ needs.build-service-images.outputs.image-ref }}`, making the job fully self-contained per matrix entry.

resolution_summary: All four review comments were addressed in uncommitted changes on the feature branch. The PR smoke test and separate smoke-test job were made matrix-aware by using `${MATRIX_SERVICE}-manage` instead of hardcoded `keystone-manage`. The smoke-test job was restructured with its own matrix strategy and independent image-ref derivation (checkout + tags step), eliminating the single-output limitation. The `wc -l` whitespace issue was fixed with `| xargs`. Beyond the directly commented locations, three additional files were updated: `docs/reference/build-images-workflow.md` was revised to document dynamic smoke test dispatch, the smoke-test matrix strategy, and updated 'Adding a New Service' instructions; `tests/container-images/verify_build_images_workflow.sh` was updated to assert dynamic `${MATRIX_SERVICE}-manage` usage and two new tests were added (`test_smoke_test_has_matrix`, `test_smoke_test_derives_image_ref`); the new `smoke-test` 'Derive image ref' step also includes the `wc -l | xargs` fix from comment [2].
